sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract and any amendments with
Benchmark Analytics (Contractor) to provide a cloud-based Early Intervention System (EIS) platform
from February 3, 2025 to February 3, 2027, in an amount not to exceed $200,000 with an option to
extend for two additional years, for a total contract amount not to exceed $400,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
These services will be funded by the General Fund in an amount not to exceed $200,000 with an
option to extend for two additional years, for a total contract amount not to exceed $400,000. This
software has existing budget approval for $100,000 per year and there are no new fiscal impacts
associated with adopting the attached resolution Funding for the extension for the additional two
years is contingent on funding being approved within the forthcoming Fiscal Year 2027 and 2028
biennial budget
sections.current_situation:
The Police Department intends to implement a comprehensive Early Intervention System platform to
proactively identify potential issues and support officer well-being and professional development. The
current system lacks automated mechanisms for department management to systematically monitor
and analyze officer performance data. A modern, data-driven platform will help reduce liability exposure
through early detection and intervention. This system reinforces departmental values, particularly our
core value of professionalism- demonstrated through progressive training, positive attitude and superior
performance.
sections.background:
As part of the Department’s commitment to the principles of fair and impartial policing, the
Department revised Policy 1041 governing the Early Warning System in 2021 to incorporate
individual officer stop data. In 2022, Council approved $100,000 for a consultant to further improve
the system. During the RFP process, the Department, the Police Accountability Board and the
Berkeley Police Association identified a need to pivot toward a comprehensive cloud-based Early
Intervention System platform. Council approved funding for this subscription service as part of the
FY25-26 budget process in June 2024.
Page 2 of 3
Internal
In September 2024, the City issued a request for proposals for an Early Intervention System platform.
A panel consisting of representatives from the Berkeley Police Department, the Police Accountability
Board, and the Berkeley Police Association selected Benchmark Analytics' proposal as the best
solution based on their research-based approach, extensive law enforcement experience, and proven
platform capabilities.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects, climate impacts, or sustainability opportunities
associated with the subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Benchmark Analytics offers a unique, evidence-based Early Intervention System built on research
partnerships with the University of Chicago. Their configurable platform will enable BPD to:
 Analyze officer performance data through advanced analytics
 Identify potential issues early through a proven risk assessment model
 Implement targeted interventions and track outcomes
 Maintain comprehensive documentation and reporting capabilities
The platform has proven success in major departments across California and the nation, including the
San Jose Police Department, San Francisco Police Department, Baltimore Police Department, and
Charlotte-Mecklenburg Police Department. The system's track record in these jurisdictions
demonstrates its effectiveness in supporting officer development and risk management in diverse
agency contexts.
sections.alternatives:
The Department considered both consulting services to improve the current system and early
intervention platforms from other vendors. After thorough evaluation, Benchmark Analytics' research-
based platform offered the most comprehensive and proven solution for BPD's needs.
CONTACT PERSON
Jen Louis, Chief of Police, (510) 981-5700
Arlo Malmberg, Strategic Planning and Accountability Manager, (510) 981-5747
sections.attachments:
1. Resolution
Page 3 of 3
ATTACHMENT 1
Page 1
RESOLUTION NO. XX,XXX-N.S.
BENCHMARK ANALYTICS FOR EARLY INTERVENTION SYSTEM PLATFORM
WHEREAS, in accordance with the principles of fair and impartial policing, the Berkeley
Police Department revised Policy 1041 governing the Early Warning System in 2021 to
incorporate individual officer stop data; and
WHEREAS, in 2022, Council approved funding to improve the Department's early
warning system capabilities through external consultation; and
WHEREAS, during the RFP process, and in accordance with the Department's
commitment to fair and impartial policing, the Department identified a need for a
comprehensive cloud-based Early Intervention System platform to provide automated
data analysis and risk assessment capabilities; and
WHEREAS, Benchmark Analytics offers a unique, research-based Early Intervention
System platform built in partnership with the University of Chicago and supported by the
National Police Early Intervention and Outcomes Research Consortium; and
WHEREAS, the Benchmark Analytics platform will provide BPD with advanced
analytics, proven risk assessment models, intervention tracking, and comprehensive
reporting capabilities; and
WHEREAS, Council approved ongoing funding for this subscription service as part of
the FY25-26 budget process in June 2024; and
WHEREAS, funds are available in the FY 2025 budget in the General Fund.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with Benchmark
Analytics (Contractor) to provide a cloud-based Early Intervention System platform from
February 3, 2025 to February 3, 2027, in an amount not to exceed $200,000 with an
option to extend for two additional years, for a total contract amount not to exceed
$400,000.
